QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_62

question.text: Using simple interest approximation, if the spot price is Rs. 10,000, risk-free rate is 7%, dividend yield is 1.5%, and time to expiry is 45 days, what is the approximate fair futures price?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Step-by-step calculation using simple interest approximation: F = S x [1 + (r - q) x T/365] F = 10,000 x [1 + (0.07 - 0.015) x 45/365] F = 10,000 x [1 + 0.055 x 0.1233] F = 10,000 x [1 + 0.006781] F = 10,000 x 1.006781 F = Rs. 10,067.81
**The Trap:** Rs. 10,550 uses full-year carry of 5.5%. Rs. 10,700 uses 7% for full year. The time fraction 45/365 is critical - the carry for 45 days is much less than a full year.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Simple interest approximation gives results close to compounding for short durations, commonly used in Series VIII.
**Key Takeaway:** Simple interest: F = S x [1 + (r-q) x T/365] = 10,000 x [1 + 0.055 x 45/365] = Rs. 10,067.81.
